# Clint McMahon

> Clint McMahon is a software developer, architect, and photographer based in Minneapolis, Minnesota. This is his personal website and photoblog.

## About

Clint McMahon builds web applications and software systems for a living, and takes photographs as a habit. He's been writing software professionally for over 15 years, primarily in .NET and JavaScript, and lives in Minneapolis where he's worked on projects ranging from state government websites to e-commerce platforms.

The photoblog at photos.clintmcmahon.com is a running record of what caught his attention — street photography, urban scenes, coffee shops, seasons, and everyday life in the midwest.

## Sites

- Main site / blog: https://clintmcmahon.com
- Photoblog: https://photos.clintmcmahon.com

---

## clintmcmahon.com — Blog & Portfolio

### Content

- Software engineering blog: tutorials, opinions, and project write-ups
- Portfolio of past work including government, nonprofit, and commercial projects
- Topics: .NET, ASP.NET Core, JavaScript, SQL Server, React, software architecture

### Machine-Readable

- Sitemap: https://clintmcmahon.com/sitemap.xml
- RSS: https://clintmcmahon.com/feed.rss

---

## photos.clintmcmahon.com — Photoblog

### Content

- Street and urban photography from Minneapolis, Minnesota
- Everyday life, coffee, nature, architecture, seasons
- One photo post per day — no curation, no themes, just what was worth keeping
- Minneapolis photography is a primary subject: downtown, neighborhoods, Lake Street, Northeast, the Mississippi, winter and summer

### Browsing

- Archive of all photos: https://photos.clintmcmahon.com/archive
- Individual photos by date: https://photos.clintmcmahon.com/YYYY-MM-DD
- Browse by tag: https://photos.clintmcmahon.com/tag/{tag}
- Common tags: minneapolis, street, downtown, nature, coffee, winter, summer, architecture

### Machine-Readable

- Sitemap: https://photos.clintmcmahon.com/sitemap.xml
- RSS feed: https://photos.clintmcmahon.com/rss

### Photo Format

Each photo page includes structured data (JSON-LD ImageObject) with title, description, date, author, and keywords.

---
